prompt: AI developer-tools and coding startups that raised $50M+ in 2025-2026 (coding agents, IDEs, code review, testing, CI). For each: name, amount, date, wedge, traction. Cite sources.
content:
**Anysphere (Cursor), Cognition (Devin), Blitzy, and Replit** are the primary AI developer-tools/coding startups that raised $50M+ (in fact, hundreds of millions each) in 2025–2026, focused on areas like coding agents, AI-native IDEs, autonomous development, code generation/editing, and related workflows (e.g., testing/deployment).[[1]](https://news.crunchbase.com/ai/anysphere-ai-coding-cursor-funding-valuation/)[[2]](https://www.cnbc.com/2025/11/13/cursor-ai-startup-funding-round-valuation.html)[[3]](https://news.crunchbase.com/ai/blitzy-funding-valuation-autonomous-software-development-vibe-coding-startups/)[[4]](https://replit.com/blog/replit-raises-400-million-dollars)

No exhaustive public list covers every minor round, but these stand out from funding reports as the major players in the specified categories. Details below (amounts approximate where reported as ranges; dates based on announcements/closes; “wedge” refers to core product focus; traction from reported metrics).

### Anysphere (Cursor) — AI-native code editor/IDE with integrated agents
- **Raises**: ~$900M Series C (May/June 2025) at ~$9–9.9B valuation; $2.3B Series D (November 2025, or late October close in some reports) at $29.3B post-money valuation. (Later 2026 talks for ~$2B at ~$50B valuation reported but not confirmed as closed here.)[[1]](https://news.crunchbase.com/ai/anysphere-ai-coding-cursor-funding-valuation/)[[2]](https://www.cnbc.com/2025/11/13/cursor-ai-startup-funding-round-valuation.html)[[5]](https://tech-insider.org/cursor-60-billion-valuation-anysphere-ai-coding-2026/)
- **Wedge**: AI-first code editor (VS Code fork) with deep agentic capabilities for code generation, multi-file edits, chat, completions, and workflows; positions itself as a full AI-native development environment.
- **Traction**: Explosive growth to $2B ARR by February 2026 (from $100M ARR in Jan 2025); fastest B2B SaaS ramp on record; adopted by major enterprises (e.g., OpenAI, Spotify, Uber, Instacart); high developer usage and model training on real code.[[6]](https://www.tryanalyze.ai/blog/fastest-growing-ai-companies)[[7]](https://mightybot.ai/blog/coding-ai-agents-for-accelerating-engineering-workflows/)

### Cognition (Devin) — Autonomous AI coding/software engineering agent
- **Raises**: $400M round (September 2025) at $10.2B valuation (post-Windsurf acquisition); >$1B round (May 2026) at ~$25–26B post-money valuation. Total funding exceeds $1.7B+ across rounds.[[8]](https://cognition.ai/blog/funding-growth-and-the-next-frontier-of-ai-coding-agents)[[9]](https://techcrunch.com/2026/05/27/ai-coding-startup-cognition-raises-1b-at-25b-pre-money-valuation/)[[10]](https://aifundingtracker.com/ai-startup-funding-news-today/)
- **Wedge**: Fully autonomous AI agent (Devin) that plans, executes, debugs, and validates multi-step software tasks on codebases; combined with Windsurf (acquired) for agentic IDE/features; focuses on end-to-end engineering autonomy.
- **Traction**: ARR grew from ~$1M (Sep 2024) to $73M (June 2025) pre-major raises, reaching $492M run-rate by May 2026 (13x YoY); strong enterprise interest in autonomous coding; valuation surge reflects agentic AI momentum.[[10]](https://aifundingtracker.com/ai-startup-funding-news-today/)[[8]](https://cognition.ai/blog/funding-growth-and-the-next-frontier-of-ai-coding-agents)

### Blitzy — Autonomous AI platform for enterprise software development
- **Raise**: $200M growth/Series B round (May 2026) at $1.4B valuation (total funding >$204M).[[3]](https://news.crunchbase.com/ai/blitzy-funding-valuation-autonomous-software-development-vibe-coding-startups/)[[11]](https://pitchbook.com/profiles/company/633169-18)
- **Wedge**: Orchestrates thousands of AI agents to understand, modify, and modernize large/legacy enterprise codebases; completes full projects with built-in testing/validation; targets “vibe coding”/autonomous outcomes for Global 2000.
- **Traction**: Early but rapid scaling in enterprise (production-ready code delivery); positioned as infrastructure play for agentic coding at scale; backed by Northzone (lead) plus Battery, PSG, etc.[[10]](https://aifundingtracker.com/ai-startup-funding-news-today/)[[12]](https://techfundingnews.com/ex-nvidia-architects-ai-coding-startup-blitzy-raises-200m-led-by-northzone-at-1-4b-valuation/)

### Replit — Cloud IDE with AI coding agent (Replit Agent)
- **Raise**: $400M Series D (March 2026) at $9B valuation (3x increase in ~6 months).[[4]](https://replit.com/blog/replit-raises-400-million-dollars)[[13]](https://www.thesaasnews.com/news/replit-secures-400m-series-d-at-9b-valuation)
- **Wedge**: Browser/cloud IDE enhanced with autonomous Replit Agent (plans, writes, tests, deploys full apps from natural language; parallel task handling and conflict resolution); broad accessibility for prototyping to production.
- **Traction**: Users at 85% of Fortune 500; on track for $1B ARR by end of 2026; 40M+ users historically; Agent 4 launch coincided with raise for faster/more capable workflows.[[4]](https://replit.com/blog/replit-raises-400-million-dollars)[[7]](https://mightybot.ai/blog/coding-ai-agents-for-accelerating-engineering-workflows/)

**Notes**: 
- These focus strictly on AI-enhanced developer tools/coding (agents, IDEs, autonomous dev). Broader AI or non-coding dev tools (e.g., pure testing/CI without coding agents) had fewer or smaller disclosed rounds matching the threshold.
- Valuations and ARR are reported figures (often post-money or run-rate); actual closed amounts can vary slightly by source. Later 2026 rounds or secondary activity (e.g., Cursor) may exist beyond these.
- Sources primarily include Crunchbase reports, company announcements, TechCrunch, CNBC, Forbes, and funding trackers. Data is current as of mid-2026 reporting.[[10]](https://aifundingtracker.com/ai-startup-funding-news-today/)[[14]](https://waveup.com/blog/top-vc-investing-in-ai/)

Other emerging or smaller players (e.g., open-source agents like Cline or niche tools) raised less or not in the $50M+ range during this window.
